meaning: Grand Old Man n.<爱尔兰>傻子
examples: We are watching the GOM as the seabed rises and becomes stretched with the pressure build up.
It will happen still with the GOM incident, because we must get in there and remove those pent up gasses.
You will see build up and increasingly stronger earthquakes, but they are not quite at the point of explosion the GOM is.
By consolidating GOM operations, ChevronTexaco will be able to reduce operating costs while leveraging Unocal's proprietary geologic data.
In the last quarter, most drillers, including Halliburton, suffered setbacks due to weather related issues in the Gulf of Mexico (GoM).
